title: calystegia macrostegia subsp. macrostegia
intro: Calystegia macrostegia subsp. macrostegia, also known as Island Morning Glory, is a perennial vine native to the Channel Islands in California, characterized by its bright purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its alkaloid content, which has been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Calystegia macrostegia subsp. macrostegia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made through drying and infusing the leaves and flowers, often used in teas, tinctures, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
